dev_banner.message: API: {apiBase} — Use "npm run dev" and open this URL; log in if you see no projects.

home.hero_subtitle: Day after day, we show up and do our part.
home.hero_body: This platform was built from real needs on the ground. Rural projects don't follow rigid templates — they need flexibility to adapt, tools to track every dollar, every hour of labor, and every outcome. Three integrated apps handle the full cycle: project management, financial oversight, and field execution.
home.how_subtitle: A transparent, algorithm-driven approach to grant management — with a human touch.
home.section1_desc: A regenerative farm and NGO platform that manages community grants with full transparency. Every project is broken into measurable steps, scored with an open algorithm, and verified with documented proof.
home.section2_desc: Every grant decision is driven by a scoring formula that balances impact, cost, and risk. The algorithm ensures objectivity while a compassion-empathy split leaves room for human judgment.
home.section3_desc: Three purpose-built web applications work together to manage grants, track finances, and coordinate fieldwork — all sharing a single database for real-time transparency.
home.section4_desc: From initial research to final verification, every project follows a structured 9-step lifecycle that ensures accountability, documentation, and measurable outcomes at every stage.
home.overview_subtitle: Three pillars that keep every dollar accountable and every decision transparent.
home.how_steps_desc: Each project is split into concrete steps with a detailed budget: monetary costs, in-kind skilled labor, community contributions, and a time estimate. Every step has a clear intervention scope and timeline.
home.overview_steps_long: Every initiative begins as a project, which is then broken into sequential steps — each with its own budget, intervention scope, and timeline. Budgets distinguish between monetary costs (materials, logistics, services), in-kind skilled labor (our volunteers' hours valued at market rate), and community labor (local people contributing physical work). Steps must be completed in order: Step 1 before Step 2, and so on. This structure ensures nothing is vague — every dollar, every hour, every material is planned before work begins.
home.how_scoring_desc: A multi-factor algorithm scores each step based on urgency, breadth and depth of impact, neglectedness, cost-effectiveness, and confidence in estimates. Higher-impact, lower-cost interventions surface first.
home.overview_scoring_long: When resources are limited, you need a way to decide what gets funded first. Our algorithm scores every eligible step across multiple dimensions — urgency, how many people benefit, how deeply it changes their lives, whether anyone else is funding it, and how confident we are in the estimates. The result is a single number that ranks steps from most impactful to least. The algorithm also factors in risk: if past steps went over budget, future estimates are adjusted upward. No human bias in the selection — just math.
home.how_verification_desc: When a step is completed, proof documents are uploaded and actual costs are recorded. Performance is measured accurately against the original budget — every dollar, every labor hour, every outcome.
home.overview_verification_long: Completion isn't just checking a box. When a step finishes, the team uploads proof documents — invoices, receipts, photos — and enters the actual costs. The system compares what was allocated against what was actually spent. If the step came in under budget, the difference is returned to the wallet. If it went over, the extra is recorded as an additional cost. Every cent is reconciled, every labor hour is logged, and the full audit trail is permanently stored.
home.algo_subtitle: // how we decide what gets funded first
home.algo_intro: With limited funds and many worthy projects, we need an objective way to prioritize. Our scoring algorithm evaluates every eligible step and produces a single number — the score — that determines funding order. The formula balances how much good a step will do against how much it costs to deliver, then adjusts for context. No politics, no favorites — just transparent math that anyone can verify.
home.algo_formula_desc: The score is a ratio: impact divided by real cost, scaled by multipliers. A step that helps many people urgently and cheaply will score higher than one that helps fewer people, less urgently, at greater expense. The ×100 is just for readability — it turns small decimals into numbers humans can compare at a glance.
home.algo_impact_desc: Impact answers: "How much good will this step do?" It multiplies three dimensions. Urgency (1–10) captures how time-sensitive the need is — a collapsing roof scores far higher than a cosmetic improvement. Breadth (1–5) measures how many people benefit, from a single individual to an entire region. Depth (1–5) measures how profoundly each person's life improves, from minor convenience to life-changing transformation. The scales are non-linear: urgency 10 is 18× more powerful than urgency 1, because a true crisis should dramatically outweigh routine maintenance.
home.algo_cost_desc: The denominator keeps the algorithm honest about expense. Base cost sums monetary costs (materials, services, logistics), in-kind labor value (our volunteers' hours at market rate), and community labor value. Risk is a learned multiplier (1.0–1.6) that penalizes uncertainty: if previous steps in similar conditions went over budget, risk rises and the score drops. External dependencies add a 10% risk penalty. The system learns from every completed step, so estimates improve over time.
home.algo_m_momentum_desc: Projects already started get a 10–20% boost. Finishing what you've begun is more efficient than starting fresh.
home.algo_m_neglect_desc: If nobody else is funding this area, the step gets up to a 30% boost. We should fill gaps, not pile onto well-funded causes.
home.algo_m_confidence_desc: A discount from 0.6 to 1.0 based on how certain we are about the estimates. Uncertain projects are penalized, not ignored.
home.algo_m_emergency_desc: True life-or-safety emergencies get a 4× multiplier that vaults them to the top of the queue, regardless of cost.
home.algo_allocation_desc: Once every eligible step is scored, they're sorted from highest to lowest. The algorithm walks down the list and allocates budget to each step until the money runs out. Only the first incomplete step of each project is considered — you can't skip ahead. If a step's monetary cost exceeds the remaining budget, it's skipped. This greedy, top-down approach ensures maximum impact per dollar spent.
home.philosophy_subtitle: If we followed pure mathematics, survival projects would always crush community morale projects. The algorithm would be efficient, but cold. So we split every grant into two pools.
home.compassion_desc: The majority of each grant is allocated automatically by the algorithm to the highest-scoring steps — prioritizing urgency, impact, and cost-effectiveness. This is the rational engine that handles crises, infrastructure, and core needs.
home.empathy_desc: A portion of each grant is reserved for the team to allocate manually. This covers projects the algorithm might undervalue — community morale, emotional well-being, things that matter but don't score high on urgency alone.
home.philosophy_footer: The ratio between Compassion and Empathy is configurable for each grant. By default, 85% goes to Compassion and 15% to Empathy.
home.stack_subtitle: // three apps, one database, zero off-the-shelf compromises
home.tech_intro: No existing tool did what we needed. Grant platforms were too rigid, finance apps didn't track labor, and project managers didn't understand field work. So we built our own — three open-source apps that share a single database and talk to each other in real time.
home.app_gm_desc: The public dashboard you're looking at right now. Tracks projects, steps, budgets, scoring, and the full grant lifecycle from backlog to completion.
home.tech_gm_long: This is the app you're looking at right now — the public-facing dashboard where every project, step, and budget lives. It manages the full grant lifecycle: creating projects with detailed budgets (monetary, in-kind labor, community contributions), scoring steps through the multi-factor algorithm, running the two-phase Compassion and Empathy allocation, and verifying completed work with proof documents. It also provides interactive maps, photo galleries, category-based filtering, a complete transaction history per step, and real-time statistics. Everything is bilingual (English and Spanish), fully themed, and designed to be transparent — anyone can see where money goes and why.
home.app_fm_desc: Where the money lives. Manages wallets, transactions, expense logging, labor tracking, and cost-type breakdowns. Every dollar in GM traces back here.
home.tech_fm_long: Every dollar that enters the system passes through Finance Manager. It handles multiple wallets (each with its own balance), deposits, donations (anonymous or public with receipt uploads), expense logging by cost type, and wallet-to-wallet transfers. Beyond cash, it tracks in-kind labor hours (our volunteers) and community labor hours (local contributions) — complete with people, hours, and hourly rates. When GM completes a step, it pulls verified costs and labor logs directly from FM, ensuring the numbers match reality. FM is mobile-first, with voice-powered transaction entry and QR code scanning, because field teams often work from phones.
home.app_pm_desc: Kanban boards for field teams. Photos, to-dos, discussions, and WhatsApp integration. Each GM step becomes a card the team works on in the real world — and stays connected to beneficiaries for long-term updates and follow-up interventions.
home.tech_pm_long: This is where the actual work happens. Every GM project syncs into PM as a Kanban board — each step becomes a card that the field team moves through columns. Cards hold checklists, threaded discussions, photo documentation, file attachments, and intervention logs. Discussions sync with WhatsApp groups so community contacts stay in the loop without needing the app. PM also handles research: new project ideas start as cards on a research board, and once confirmed, they're imported into GM as real projects. An AI assistant helps with meeting transcription, step completion reports, and quick lookups across the system.
home.tech_glue_desc: All three apps read and write to the same database in real time. When GM creates a project, PM instantly sees it as a Kanban board. When FM logs a transaction against a step, GM can pull those numbers during verification. When PM marks a card complete, it can trigger the step completion flow in GM. There are no CSV exports, no manual syncing, no copy-paste between apps — they're three views of the same truth, each optimized for a different job.
home.tech_glue_footer: Three apps, one PocketBase instance, real-time sync via WebSockets. Open source, self-hosted, no vendor lock-in.
home.cta_sub: Browse all projects, see their steps, budgets, and progress.
home.road_subtitle: // from idea to impact, step by step
home.road_1_desc: A need surfaces through community input, partner feedback, or team observation
home.road_2_desc: Steps, budgets, and target locations are defined in the system
home.road_3_desc: Each step is ranked across 12 metrics including impact, cost, and urgency
home.road_4_desc: Resources flow through Compassion and Empathy pools; money is reserved from wallets
home.road_5_desc: The team deploys on-site with tasks, photos, and direct beneficiary collaboration
home.road_6_desc: Expenses, labor hours, and in-kind contributions are recorded in real time
home.road_7_desc: Proof documents are uploaded, actual costs reconciled, and the step is closed
home.road_8_desc: Once every step is verified and closed, the project is marked complete automatically
home.road_9_desc: Long-term follow-up with beneficiaries ensures ongoing updates and support

dashboard.map_description: Project locations on the map. Use the filter to show all projects, only completed (all steps done), in progress (at least one step in progress), or backlog (no steps started). Click a marker and "See Details" to open the project.
dashboard.financial_history_desc: Deposits: money in (any wallet). Cash: monetary costs of steps completed in each period. In-Kind: in-kind labor value of steps completed in the period plus scholarships. Cumulative: running total of Cash + In-Kind (matches "Donated + In-Kind" at the end).
dashboard.grants_by_category_desc: Total monetary costs plus in-kind labor value for all completed steps, by project category.
dashboard.value_cost_structure_desc: Monetary costs only (no in-kind, no community labor) for all completed steps, split by cost type as in step creation.
dashboard.bubble_chart_desc: Each dot is one completed step. Horizontal axis: project category. Vertical axis: total step value (monetary + in-kind + community labor). Click a dot to open the project.
dashboard.funding_gap_desc: Projects with at least one step not yet completed. Each bar: green = value of completed steps (funded); yellow = remaining value (gap). Total = monetary + in-kind + community for all steps. Click a project name to open it.
dashboard.our_effort_desc: Total skilled labor donated by our interns
dashboard.leverage_performance_desc: Our objective is to have at least 33% of total hours (ours + community) come from the community. When community contributes a meaningful share of the work, participation is more meaningful, ownership is stronger, and outcomes are more sustainable. The bar below shows how close we are to that target.

stats.funding_velocity_desc: Projects further right were funded faster. Size represents total cost.
stats.spending_by_cost_type_desc: Verified monetary spending from completed steps, broken down by cost type (assets, services, logistics, etc.).
stats.monthly_cash_flow_desc: Last 12 months: money in (deposits/donations) vs out (allocations, reimbursements).
stats.projects_by_step_count_desc: How many projects have 1, 2, or 3 steps (non-scholarship only).
stats.category_completion_stack_desc: Completed vs remaining projects per category.
stats.value_distribution_desc: How many completed projects fall in each value band ($).
stats.transaction_mix_desc: Share of transactions by type (Deposits, Allocations, Reimbursements, etc.).
stats.allocation_vs_verified_step_desc: Each point is a step. Both axes use total value (monetary + in-kind + community). Points on the diagonal = verified total matches allocated total.
stats.allocation_vs_verified_zones_desc: Same data as above. Diagonal = on budget. Pale green = under budget (verified < allocated); pale red = over budget (verified > allocated).
stats.on_budget_by_category_desc: Per category: share of completed value (total step value) where verified was within 10% of allocated. 100% = all completed value on budget.
stats.where_money_went_desc: Share of total delivered value (completed projects) per category.
stats.cumulative_completions_desc: Running total of completed projects by completion month.
stats.pipeline_step_status_desc: All steps across all projects: how many are still in backlog, in progress, or completed.
stats.money_out_vs_back_desc: Total allocated to steps vs total reimbursed (savings returned to wallet).
stats.ngo_vs_community_hours_desc: Polar comparison: your volunteer hours vs community-contributed hours across completed work.
stats.how_money_flows_title: How Money Flows Through the System
stats.lifecycle_from_creation: Complete lifecycle from project creation to completion
stats.creation_new_project: New project added with title, description, location
stats.creation_steps_defined: Steps defined with cost breakdowns & utility scores
stats.allocation_frozen_desc: Funds are frozen from wallet (can't be used elsewhere)
stats.allocation_hourglass_desc: Step moves to verification queue, shows hourglass
stats.verification_enter_costs: Enter actual costs (may differ from allocated)
stats.step_completed_desc1: Completion checkbox checked or funded ≥ cost
stats.step_completed_desc2: Shows green checkmark, PDF icon for proof
stats.step2_available: Now Step 2 becomes available for grants!
stats.project_completed_desc1: All steps verified with proof documents
stats.project_completed_desc2: Project moves from Backlog to Completed page
stats.project_completed_desc3: Stats & reports updated with final values
stats.impact_counted: Project impact counted in community statistics!
stats.divisible_steps_desc: For divisible steps, steps 2-3 can repeat multiple times until the step's full value is funded:
stats.funded_field_note: Each verification creates its own transaction. The funded field accumulates across verifications.
stats.inkind_same_flow: In-kind projects follow the exact same two-phase system, but also track:
stats.realtime_visualization_desc: Real-time visualization of how funds move through your organization

score_explanation.title: How the Score Works
score_explanation.the_idea_text: Imagine having limited funds for many projects. You want to choose the ones that do the most good and have the best chance of success, without spending too much. The formula creates a number (the score) to rank steps from most important to least important.
score_explanation.higher_score_note: The higher the score, the more that step should be funded before others.
score_explanation.formula_impact_desc: How much good this step does (importance)
score_explanation.formula_cost_desc: How much it "really costs" to do (money + risk)
score_explanation.formula_multipliers_desc: Adjustments for momentum, neglectedness, confidence, and emergencies
score_explanation.urgency_desc: Is it urgent to do this now?
score_explanation.depth_desc: How much does it change lives?
score_explanation.scales_nonlinear: The scales are non-linear: higher values weigh much more. A safety issue almost always beats a convenience.
score_explanation.real_cost_denominator: The denominator says: "how much does it cost to achieve that impact".
score_explanation.base_cost_bullet: Base Cost: Money + labor value (even if not paid in cash)
score_explanation.risk_bullet: Risk: Penalty if cost/time overruns are likely
score_explanation.momentum_detail: Bonus for projects already started (Step 2+ gets 1.1-1.2×)
score_explanation.neglectedness_detail: Bonus if no one else will fund this
score_explanation.confidence_detail: Discount if we're not sure it'll work (0.6-1.0)
score_explanation.rule_one_step: Only one step per project is eligible (the first incomplete one)
score_explanation.rule_steps_order: Steps must be done in order: Step 1 → Step 2 → Step 3
score_explanation.rule_score_locked: When a step is started, its score is locked
score_explanation.rule_risk_learns: Risk learns: after each completed step, estimates vs reality updates future risk
score_explanation.simple_analogy_text: You have 10 cookies to share. Give cookies first to who is most hungry (urgency), then to who will share with siblings (breadth), then to who really needs them to feel better (depth). If delivering cookies to someone risks losing them (risk), maybe start with who is closer.
score_explanation.weights_intro: These tables show the weight mappings and how each parameter affects the final score.
score_explanation.urgency_table_note: Non-linear: higher values have disproportionately more weight
score_explanation.depth_table_note: How much it changes each person's life (1=minor, 5=transformative)
score_explanation.neglectedness_table_note: How underfunded the area is (1=well-funded, 5=severely neglected)
score_explanation.momentum_table_note: Bonus for projects already in progress
score_explanation.score_calculator_intro: Experiment with parameters to see how they affect the final score.
score_explanation.comparison_intro: Compared to our current backlog (real data — {total} steps across {totalProjects} projects):

empathy_explanation.title: Compassionate & Empathic Giving
empathy_explanation.philosophy_p1: Our approach to giving combines two complementary strategies: Compassionate Giving (following the numbers) and Empathic Giving (following the heart).
empathy_explanation.philosophy_p2: By default, we allocate 85% of funds to high-impact projects selected by our scoring algorithm, and 15% to projects that may not score highest but bring joy, beauty, or meaning to communities.
empathy_explanation.compassionate_item1: Uses the scoring algorithm to prioritize projects
empathy_explanation.compassionate_item2: Focuses on urgent needs, high beneficiary counts, and cost-effectiveness
empathy_explanation.compassionate_item3: Ensures most resources go where they'll do the most measurable good
empathy_explanation.compassionate_item4: Projects are selected automatically based on score ranking
empathy_explanation.empathic_goal: Support projects that enrich community life beyond pure metrics.
empathy_explanation.empathic_item1: Reserved for projects that might never top the algorithm
empathy_explanation.empathic_item2: Things like murals, gardens, cultural events, playgrounds
empathy_explanation.empathic_item3: Selected manually from the lowest-scoring eligible projects
empathy_explanation.empathic_item4: Acknowledges that not everything valuable can be measured
empathy_explanation.why_both_p1: If we only followed the algorithm, important but "less urgent" projects would never get funded. A community might get clean water (high score) but never a playground (low score).
empathy_explanation.why_both_p2: The 85/15 split ensures we're being responsible stewards of resources while also making space for the intangible things that make communities thrive.
empathy_explanation.purple_star_note: Whenever you see a purple star next to a tier, it means that step was manually selected as part of our empathic giving initiative.
